mnemonics: KISS: Key, Iterator, Set, Sorting. The fundamentals for traversing and ordering a Map.
keyPoints: Maps store key-value pairs, keys are unique, values can be duplicated
Use HashMap for performance, TreeMap for sorted order, LinkedHashMap for insertion-order iteration
The entrySet() method is the most efficient way to iterate over both keys and values
commonMistakes: Using a mutable object as a key without overriding hashCode() and equals()
ConcurrentModificationException when modifying a Map during iteration without using an Iterator's remove method
Assuming null keys or values are allowed (e.g., TreeMap doesn't allow null keys, Hashtable doesn't allow null keys/values)
